meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Speed Up Freelance Dev Workflows
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speeds by mastering AI-driven debugging. A beginner's guide to using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver projects faster.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing ghost bugs manually. By integrating AI-assisted debugging, you offload the cognitive load of stack trace analysis to agents. Using Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for surgical code fixes, you can reduce your debugging cycle by 70%, allowing you to ship features faster and impress clients.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: it is 2 AM, the client demo is in six hours, and a cryptic error is bricking your production build. You are staring at a stack trace that looks like static, wasting hours digging through legacy code. This is the bottleneck that kills freelance profit margins and turns dream projects into nightmares.

The secret to scaling as a solo dev isn't writing faster; it is automating the noise. By leveraging AI-native workflows, you shift from manual hunting to orchestrating solutions. Let’s turn your debugging process from a liability into your most powerful competitive advantage.

steps.title: Contextualize the Error
steps.description: Before fixing, you must provide the AI with the complete scope of the failure. Feed the relevant logs and source files to establish a baseline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached stack trace and identify the root cause in the source code. Output a concise summary of the logic failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read src/components/auth.ts --query 'Why is this throwing a 401 error?'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map the dependencies and identify if the bug is caused by a recent refactor or a stale environment variable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the call stack for the current error. Identify any conflicts with existing dependencies or configuration files.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add logs/error.log && claude run 'find the pattern in these logs'

steps.title: Surgical Code Injection
steps.description: Once the issue is identified, use Claude Code to generate the specific fix, ensuring it adheres to your existing project architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactor for the identified bug that maintains compatibility with the existing API structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement the fix suggested in the logs to src/utils/api.ts'

steps.title: Validation and Testing
steps.description: Never trust an automated fix blindly. Use AI to write a unit test that verifies the bug is squashed and won't regress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that triggers the previously identified bug. Verify that the fix passes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a test file to verify the fix and run it'

steps.title: Deployment Verification
steps.description: Final check before pushing to production. Ensure all environment-specific variables are accounted for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the final code changes for any potential production environment risks or performance regressions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'fix: resolve authentication race condition'

pro_tips: Always include your package.json in the initial context window to prevent the AI from suggesting deprecated library methods.
Use 'Claude Code' in a dedicated terminal pane to keep your main editor workspace clean of AI-generated noise.
Tag your error logs with timestamps and severity levels; AI models perform 30% better when logs are structured as JSON.
If the AI enters a loop, explicitly command it to 'ignore previous assumptions' to force a fresh architectural perspective.
Create a custom 'debug-context' file in your repo that lists your project's specific constraints and coding standards for the AI to follow.
content.next_steps: Integrate these tools into your CI/CD pipeline to automate the initial triage of incoming bug reports.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to stop fighting your codebase and start shipping features at velocity. Stop wasting hours in the trenches and start leading with AI-powered precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Before fixing, you must provide the AI with the complete scope of the failure. Feed the relevant logs and source files to establish a baseline.
Use Antigravity to map the dependencies and identify if the bug is caused by a recent refactor or a stale environment variable.
Once the issue is identified, use Claude Code to generate the specific fix, ensuring it adheres to your existing project architecture.
Never trust an automated fix blindly. Use AI to write a unit test that verifies the bug is squashed and won't regress.
Final check before pushing to production. Ensure all environment-specific variables are accounted for.
